create_article.description: You can create a new article by making a POST request to `https://api.intercom.io/articles`. > 📘 Tags cannot be managed via the Articles API > > Article tags are read-only in responses. To creat...

author_id.description: The id of the author of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the id of the author of the default language's content. Must be a teammate on the help center's workspace.

body.description: The content of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the body of the default language's content.

description.description: The description of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the description of the default language's content.

parent_id.description: The id of the article's parent collection or section. An article without this field stands alone.

parent_type.description: The type of parent, which can either be a `collection` or `section`.

state.description: Whether the article will be `published` or will be a `draft`. Defaults to draft. For multilingual articles, this will be the state of the default language's content.

title.description: The title of the article.For multilingual articles, this will be the title of the default language's content.

translated_content.description: The Translated Content of an Article. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the article.

create_collection.description: You can create a new collection by making a POST request to `https://api.intercom.io/help_center/collections.`

description.description: The description of the collection. For multilingual collections, this will be the description of the default language's content.

help_center_id.description: The id of the help center where the collection will be created. If `null` then it will be created in the default help center.

name.description: The name of the collection. For multilingual collections, this will be the name of the default language's content.

parent_id.description: The id of the parent collection. If `null` then it will be created as the first level collection.

translated_content.description: The Translated Content of an Group. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the Group.

email.description: An email address for your user. An email should only be used where your application uses email to uniquely identify users.

id.description: The unique identifier for the contact (lead or user) which is given by Intercom.

create_external_page.display_name: Create an external page (or update an external page by external ID)
create_external_page.description: You can create a new external page by sending a POST request to this endpoint. If an external page already exists with the specified source_id and external_id, it will be updated instead.

ai_agent_availability.description: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by AI Agent. Will not default when updating an existing external page.

ai_copilot_availability.description: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by AI Copilot. Will not default when updating an existing external page.

external_id.description: The identifier for the external page which was given by the source. Must be unique for the source.

html.description: The body of the external page in HTML.

source_id.description: The unique identifier for the source of the external page which was given by Intercom. Every external page must be associated with a Content Import Source which represents the place it comes from a...

title.description: The title of the external page.

url.description: The URL of the external page. This will be used by Fin to link end users to the page it based its answer on. When a URL is not present, Fin will not reference the source.

body.description: The content of the message. HTML and plaintext are supported.

from.description: The sender of the message. If not provided, the default sender will be used.

template.description: The style of the outgoing message. Possible values `plain` or `personal`.

state.description: News items will not be visible to your users in the assigned newsfeeds until they are set live.

monthly_spend.description: How much revenue the company generates for your business. Note that this will truncate floats. i.e. it only allow for whole integers, 155.98 will be truncated to 155. Note that this has an upper li...

merge_contact.display_name: Merge a lead and a user
merge_contact.description: You can merge a contact with a `role` of `lead` into a contact with a `role` of `user`.

from.description: The unique identifier for the contact to merge away from. Must be a lead.

into.description: The unique identifier for the contact to merge into. Must be a user.

notify_attribute_collected.display_name: Notify Intercom of an attribute collector response in a custom channel conver...
notify_attribute_collected.description: Notifies Intercom that a user provided a response to an attribute collector in your custom channel/platform. This allows Intercom to process the attribute and trigger any relevant workflow automati...

notify_new_conversation.display_name: Notify Intercom of a new conversation created in a custom channel
notify_new_conversation.description: Notifies Intercom that a new conversation was created in your custom channel/platform. This triggers conversation creation and workflow automations within Intercom for your custom channel integrati...

external_conversation_id.description: Identifier for the conversation in your application.

notify_new_message.display_name: Notify Intercom of a new message in a custom channel conversation
notify_new_message.description: Notifies Intercom that a new message was sent in a conversation on your custom channel/platform. This allows Intercom to process the message and trigger any relevant workflow automations. > **Note:...

notify_quick_reply_selected.display_name: Notify Intercom of a quick reply response in a custom channel conversation
notify_quick_reply_selected.description: Notifies Intercom that a user selected a quick reply option in your custom channel/platform. This allows Intercom to process the response and trigger any relevant workflow automations. > **Note:** ...

post_export_reporting_data_enqueue.display_name: Enqueue a new reporting data export job
post_export_reporting_data_enqueue.description: Enqueue a new reporting data export job

redact_conversation.description: You can redact a conversation part or the source message of a conversation (as seen in the source object). {% admonition type="info" name="Redacting parts and messages" %} If you are redacting a co...

register_fin_voice_call.description: Register a Fin Voice call with Intercom. This endpoint creates an external reference that links an external call identifier to an Intercom call and conversation. The call can be from different sour...

call_id.description: External call identifier from the call provider

phone_number.description: Phone number in E.164 format for the call

source.description: Source of the call. Can be "five9", "zoom_phone", or defaults to "aws_connect"

reply_conversation.description: You can reply to a conversation with a message from an admin or on behalf of a contact, or with a note for admins.

conversation_id.description: The Intercom provisioned identifier for the conversation or the string "last" to reply to the last part of the conversation

reply_ticket.description: You can reply to a ticket with a message from an admin or on behalf of a contact, or with a note for admins.

skip_notifications.description: Option to disable notifications when replying to a Ticket.

retrieve_a_company_by_id.description: You can fetch a single company.

company_id.description: The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom

retrieve_admin.description: You can retrieve the details of a single admin.

admin_id.description: The unique identifier of a given admin

retrieve_article.description: You can fetch the details of a single article by making a GET request to `https://api.intercom.io/articles/<id>`.

article_id.description: The unique identifier for the article which is given by Intercom.

retrieve_brand.description: Fetches a specific brand by its unique identifier

id.description: The unique identifier of the brand

retrieve_collection.description: You can fetch the details of a single collection by making a GET request to `https://api.intercom.io/help_center/collections/<id>`.

collection_id.description: The unique identifier for the collection which is given by Intercom.

retrieve_company.description: You can fetch a single company by passing in `company_id` or `name`. `https://api.intercom.io/companies?name={name}` `https://api.intercom.io/companies?company_id={company_id}` You can fetch all co...

company_id.description: The `company_id` of the company to filter by.

name.description: The `name` of the company to filter by.

page.description: The page of results to fetch. Defaults to first page

per_page.description: How many results to display per page. Defaults to 15

segment_id.description: The `segment_id` of the company to filter by.

tag_id.description: The `tag_id` of the company to filter by.

retrieve_conversation.description: You can fetch the details of a single conversation. This will return a single Conversation model with all its conversation parts. {% admonition type="warning" name="Hard limit of 500 parts" %} The ...

conversation_id.description: The id of the conversation to target

display_as.description: Set to plaintext to retrieve conversation messages in plain text.

include_translations.description: If set to true, conversation parts will be translated to the detected language of the conversation.

retrieve_email.description: Fetches a specific email setting by its unique identifier

id.description: The unique identifier of the email setting

retrieve_help_center.description: You can fetch the details of a single Help Center by making a GET request to `https://api.intercom.io/help_center/help_center/<id>`.

help_center_id.description: The unique identifier for the collection which is given by Intercom.

retrieve_internal_article.description: You can fetch the details of a single internal article by making a GET request to `https://api.intercom.io/internal_articles/<id>`.

internal_article_id.description: The unique identifier for the article which is given by Intercom.

retrieve_news_item.description: You can fetch the details of a single news item.

news_item_id.description: The unique identifier for the news item which is given by Intercom.

retrieve_newsfeed.description: You can fetch the details of a single newsfeed

newsfeed_id.description: The unique identifier for the news feed item which is given by Intercom.

retrieve_note.description: You can fetch the details of a single note.

note_id.description: The unique identifier of a given note

retrieve_segment.description: You can fetch the details of a single segment.

segment_id.description: The unique identified of a given segment.

retrieve_team.description: You can fetch the details of a single team, containing an array of admins that belong to this team.

team_id.description: The unique identifier of a given team.

retrieve_visitor_with_user_id.display_name: Retrieve a visitor with User ID
retrieve_visitor_with_user_id.description: You can fetch the details of a single visitor.

user_id.description: The user_id of the Visitor you want to retrieve.

scroll_over_all_companies.description: The `list all companies` functionality does not work well for huge datasets, and can result in errors and performance problems when paging deeply. The Scroll API provides an efficient mechanism for...

search_articles.description: You can search for articles by making a GET request to `https://api.intercom.io/articles/search`.

help_center_id.description: The ID of the Help Center to search in.

highlight.description: Return a highlighted version of the matching content within your articles. Refer to the response schema for more details.

phrase.description: The phrase within your articles to search for.

state.description: The state of the Articles returned. One of `published`, `draft` or `all`.

search_contacts.description: You can search for multiple contacts by the value of their attributes in order to fetch exactly who you want. To search for contacts, you need to send a `POST` request to `https://api.intercom.io/c...

search_conversations.description: You can search for multiple conversations by the value of their attributes in order to fetch exactly which ones you want. To search for conversations, you need to send a `POST` request to `https://...

search_internal_articles.description: You can search for internal articles by making a GET request to `https://api.intercom.io/internal_articles/search`.

folder_id.description: The ID of the folder to search in.

search_tickets.description: You can search for multiple tickets by the value of their attributes in order to fetch exactly which ones you want. To search for tickets, you send a `POST` request to `https://api.intercom.io/tick...

set_away_admin.description: You can set an Admin as away for the Inbox.

admin_id.description: The unique identifier of a given admin

away_mode_enabled.description: Set to "true" to change the status of the admin to away.

away_mode_reassign.description: Set to "true" to assign any new conversation replies to your default inbox.

away_status_reason_id.description: The unique identifier of the away status reason

show_call.description: Retrieve a single call by id.

call_id.description: The id of the call to retrieve

show_call_recording.display_name: Get call recording by call id
show_call_recording.description: Redirects to a signed URL for the call's recording if it exists.

show_call_transcript.display_name: Get call transcript by call id
show_call_transcript.description: Returns the transcript for the specified call as a downloadable text file.

show_contact.description: You can fetch the details of a single contact.

show_contact_by_external_id.display_name: Get a contact by External ID
show_contact_by_external_id.description: You can fetch the details of a single contact by external ID. Note that this endpoint only supports users and not leads.

external_id.description: The external ID of the user that you want to retrieve

unarchive_contact.description: You can unarchive a single contact.

update_article.description: You can update the details of a single article by making a PUT request to `https://api.intercom.io/articles/<id>`. > 📘 Tags cannot be managed via the Articles API > > Article tags are read-only ...

article_id.description: The unique identifier for the article which is given by Intercom.

author_id.description: The id of the author of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the id of the author of the default language's content. Must be a teammate on the help center's workspace.

body.description: The content of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the body of the default language's content.

description.description: The description of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the description of the default language's content.

parent_id.description: The id of the article's parent collection or section. An article without this field stands alone.

parent_type.description: The type of parent, which can either be a `collection` or `section`.

state.description: Whether the article will be `published` or will be a `draft`. Defaults to draft. For multilingual articles, this will be the state of the default language's content.

title.description: The title of the article.For multilingual articles, this will be the title of the default language's content.

translated_content.description: The Translated Content of an Article. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the article.

update_collection.description: You can update the details of a single collection by making a PUT request to `https://api.intercom.io/collections/<id>`.

collection_id.description: The unique identifier for the collection which is given by Intercom.

description.description: The description of the collection. For multilingual collections, this will be the description of the default language's content.

name.description: The name of the collection. For multilingual collections, this will be the name of the default language's content.

parent_id.description: The id of the parent collection. If `null` then it will be updated as the first level collection.

translated_content.description: The Translated Content of an Group. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the Group.

update_company.description: You can update a single company using the Intercom provisioned `id`. {% admonition type="warning" name="Using `company_id`" %} When updating a company it is not possible to update `company_id`. Thi...

company_id.description: The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom

custom_attributes.description: A hash of key/value pairs containing any other data about the company you want Intercom to store.

industry.description: The industry that this company operates in.

monthly_spend.description: How much revenue the company generates for your business. Note that this will truncate floats. i.e. it only allow for whole integers, 155.98 will be truncated to 155. Note that this has an upper li...

plan.description: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

size.description: The number of employees in this company.

website.description: The URL for this company's website. Please note that the value specified here is not validated. Accepts any string.

update_contact.description: You can update an existing contact (ie. user or lead). {% admonition type="info" %} This endpoint handles both **contact updates** and **custom object associations**. See _`update a contact with an...

update_content_import_source.description: You can update an existing content import source.

source_id.description: The unique identifier for the content import source which is given by Intercom.

status.description: The status of the content import source.

sync_behavior.description: If you intend to create or update External Pages via the API, this should be set to `api`. You can not change the value to or from api.

url.description: The URL of the content import source. This may only be different from the existing value if the sync behavior is API.

update_conversation.description: You can update an existing conversation. {% admonition type="info" name="Replying and other actions" %} If you want to reply to a coveration or take an action such as assign, unassign, open, close ...

company_id.description: The ID of the company that the conversation is associated with. The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom. Set to nil to remove company.

conversation_id.description: The id of the conversation to target

custom_attributes.description: An object containing the different custom attributes associated to the conversation as key-value pairs. For relationship attributes the value will be a list of custom object instance models.

display_as.description: Set to plaintext to retrieve conversation messages in plain text.

read.description: Mark a conversation as read within Intercom.

title.description: The title given to the conversation

update_data_attribute.description: You can update a data attribute. > 🚧 Updating the data type is not possible > > It is currently a dangerous action to execute changing a data attribute's type via the API. You will need to updat...

archived.description: Whether the attribute is to be archived or not.

description.description: The readable description you see in the UI for the attribute.

messenger_writable.description: Can this attribute be updated by the Messenger

update_external_page.description: You can update an existing external page (if it was created via the API).

external_id.description: The identifier for the external page which was given by the source. Must be unique for the source.

fin_availability.description: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by Fin.

html.description: The body of the external page in HTML.

page_id.description: The unique identifier for the external page which is given by Intercom.

source_id.description: The unique identifier for the source of the external page which was given by Intercom. Every external page must be associated with a Content Import Source which represents the place it comes from a...

title.description: The title of the external page.

url.description: The URL of the external page. This will be used by Fin to link end users to the page it based its answer on.

update_internal_article.description: You can update the details of a single internal article by making a PUT request to `https://api.intercom.io/internal_articles/<id>`.

author_id.description: The id of the author of the article.

internal_article_id.description: The unique identifier for the internal article which is given by Intercom.

owner_id.description: The id of the author of the article.

update_ip_allowlist.description: Update the IP allowlist configuration for the workspace. {% admonition type="warning" name="Lockout Protection" %} The API will reject updates that would lock out the caller's IP address. Ensure yo...

enabled.description: Whether the IP allowlist is enabled for the workspace.

ip_allowlist.description:
List of allowed IP addresses and/or IP ranges in CIDR notation.
Examples:
- Single IP: `192.168.0.1`
- IP range: `192.168.0.1/24` (allows 192.168.0.0 - 192.168.0.255)

type.description: String representing the object's type. Always has the value `ip_allowlist`.

body.description: The news item body, which may contain HTML.

deliver_silently.description: When set to `true`, the news item will appear in the messenger newsfeed without showing a notification badge.

labels.description: Label names displayed to users to categorize the news item.

news_item_id.description: The unique identifier for the news item which is given by Intercom.

newsfeed_assignments.description: A list of newsfeed_assignments to assign to the specified newsfeed.

reactions.description: Ordered list of emoji reactions to the news item. When empty, reactions are disabled.

sender_id.description: The id of the sender of the news item. Must be a teammate on the workspace.

state.description: News items will not be visible to your users in the assigned newsfeeds until they are set live.

title.description: The title of the news item.

skip_notifications.description: Option to disable notifications when a Ticket is updated.

update_ticket_type.description: You can update a ticket type. > 📘 Updating a ticket type. > > For the `icon` propery, use an emoji from [Twemoji Cheatsheet](https://twemoji-cheatsheet.vercel.app/)

archived.description: The archived status of the ticket type.

description.description: The description of the ticket type.

icon.description: The icon of the ticket type.

is_internal.description: Whether the tickets associated with this ticket type are intended for internal use only or will be shared with customers. This is currently a limited attribute.

name.description: The name of the ticket type.

ticket_type_id.description: The unique identifier for the ticket type which is given by Intercom.

update_ticket_type_attribute.display_name: Update an existing attribute for a ticket type
update_ticket_type_attribute.description: You can update an existing attribute for a ticket type.

allow_multiple_values.description: Whether the attribute allows multiple files to be attached to it (only applicable to file attributes)

archived.description: Whether the attribute should be archived and not shown during creation of the ticket (it will still be present on previously created tickets)

attribute_id.description: The unique identifier for the ticket type attribute which is given by Intercom.

description.description: The description of the attribute presented to the teammate or contact

list_items.description: A comma delimited list of items for the attribute value (only applicable to list attributes)

multiline.description: Whether the attribute allows multiple lines of text (only applicable to string attributes)

name.description: The name of the ticket type attribute

required_to_create.description: Whether the attribute is required to be filled in when teammates are creating the ticket in Inbox.

required_to_create_for_contacts.description: Whether the attribute is required to be filled in when contacts are creating the ticket in Messenger.

ticket_type_id.description: The unique identifier for the ticket type which is given by Intercom.

visible_on_create.description: Whether the attribute is visible to teammates when creating a ticket in Inbox.

visible_to_contacts.description: Whether the attribute is visible to contacts when creating a ticket in Messenger.

update_visitor.description: Sending a PUT request to `/visitors` will result in an update of an existing Visitor. **Option 1.** You can update a visitor by passing in the `user_id` of the visitor in the Request body. **Option...

custom_attributes.description: The custom attributes which are set for the visitor.

id.description: A unique identified for the visitor which is given by Intercom.

user_id.description: A unique identified for the visitor which is given by you.
